mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in > 65% of instances when UNRATE rises > 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising retail sales and declining interest rates amplifies consumer purchasing power and corporate revenue growth, producing strongly positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series baselines, or 60-day returns are negative when both conditions align in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which triggers short-term positive S&P 500 returns as markets price in improved economic momentum.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict 5-day returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 85%.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion and rising corporate profit margins, which consistently generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day equity returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally on inflation prints above 0.4% MoM, or 20-day returns remain positive in > 70% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms synchronized labor and construction sector expansion, validating economic resilience and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The dual-series signal produces negative 20-day returns in > 55% of aligned observations, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy drops below random chance.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in housing starts signals renewed construction activity and upstream supply chain demand, which generates immediate positive S&P 500 returns as markets anticipate near-term economic acceleration.
failure_condition: Housing starts spikes fail to predict 5-day returns, or the signal produces negative returns in > 60% of triggered cases during recovery phases.
